title: Blotterbook — improvement backlog (paste-ready prompts)
intro: From the 2026-06-25 audit. Each item is a self-contained prompt: scope, goal, constraints, "done when".
tags: Tags: P1 do-soon · P2 durable win · P3 polish. Send them one at a time (one surface/concern per turn).
note: Committed source of truth for the engineering backlog (replaces the retired .claude/backlog.md). The admin panel shows category/effort/status only; prompt + doneNote stay here.

items.title: Close the CSV→HTML injection (XSS)
items.prompt: Sanitize the futures symbol so a crafted CSV symbol can't inject HTML. In `app/adapters.js`, `rootSym()` (~line 48) currently lets arbitrary characters through; restrict its output to a safe charset (e.g. `[A-Z0-9._-]`). Verify the raw symbol is never rendered unescaped either — check the interpolation points in `app/app.js` (~493 live cost table, ~1164 exported report written via `w.document.write`, ~1385/1388 data-manager rows, ~1421 trade editor) and apply the existing `esc()` where a raw symbol still reaches `innerHTML`/`document.write`. Don't touch trade math. Done when: importing a CSV whose symbol contains `` renders inert text everywhere (dashboard, data manager, exported report) and adapter tests still pass.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `rootSym()` (`app/adapters.js:52`) now strips to `[A-Z0-9._-]` at the source, and `esc()` was applied at every sink (`app/app.js` cost table ~493, exported report ~1165, data-manager rows ~1385/1388, trade editor ~1421 — incl. `side`). Verified in-browser: `rootSym('MES')` → `MESIMGSRCXONERRORALERT1`; a stored payload renders as escaped text (no injected element, XSS never fired); legit symbols (MES/MNQ/MCL) still render; 55/55 adapter tests pass.

items.title: Add a Pages `_headers` file with CSP + security headers
items.prompt: Add a Cloudflare Pages `_headers` file applying security headers site-wide: a Content-Security-Policy (the app has no runtime deps, so aim for `default-src 'self'`; allow only what the pages actually need — inline styles/SVG, the mailto/Stripe later), plus `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`, and `X-Frame-Options: DENY`. Don't break the existing inline `<style>`/`<script>` blocks or the data-URL screenshots. Done when: response headers carry the CSP + the three hardening headers, and the app, demo, staging, admin, and marketing pages all still render and function (verify in the preview).
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** root `_headers` file applies to `/*`: CSP (`default-src 'self'`; `img-src 'self' data:` for screenshots; `connect-src 'self'`; `object-src 'none'`; `base-uri 'self'`; `form-action 'self'`; `frame-ancestors 'none'`; no `'unsafe-eval'`), plus `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`, `Permissions-Policy` (camera/mic/geo/ topics off). `script-src`/`style-src` keep `'unsafe-inline'` (3 first-party inline scripts on home/ admin/changelog + inline `style=""` attrs; no build step for nonces yet — tighten via A1/A2). NOTE: `_headers` only takes effect on Cloudflare Pages, not the local static preview — confirm the response headers on the next deploy. App/demo/staging/home/admin all still render.

items.title: Replace the raw-ADMIN_KEY-to-browser flow with a short-lived signed token
items.prompt: Right now `/api/admin-key` returns the raw `ADMIN_KEY` and the admin page caches it in localStorage, and the staging gate carries the raw key in a cookie. Replace this with a short-lived signed token: the Access-authenticated admin endpoint issues a time-limited HMAC token (signed with a server secret), the admin page stores the token (not the key), and `/api/status`, `/api/config`, and the staging gate verify the token instead of comparing the raw key. Keep raw-key acceptance as a fallback if you want. Done when: the raw `ADMIN_KEY` never reaches the browser, admin writes + staging launch still work behind Access, and tokens expire.

items.title: Verify the Cloudflare Access JWT signature (defense-in-depth)
items.prompt: `functions/api/admin-key.js` currently trusts the *presence* of the `Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion` header. Validate the JWT's signature against Cloudflare Access's JWKS (team domain + AUD) before returning anything, so the endpoint is safe even if it's ever reached off the Access-protected host. Cache the JWKS. Keep the existing 401 behavior off-Access. Done when: a request with a forged/absent assertion is rejected, a genuine Access request succeeds, and the admin page still auto-fills.

items.title: Cap per-trade screenshot size
items.prompt: Screenshots are stored as data-URLs in IndexedDB (`trademeta`). Add a per-image size cap (e.g. reject or downscale > ~1–2 MB) with a friendly message in the trade editor, so the local DB can't balloon. Done when: oversized images are downscaled or refused with a clear note; existing data is untouched.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `dmAddShot` (`app/datamanager.js`) now auto-downscales (longest side ≤ 1600px) and re-encodes to JPEG q0.82 whenever an image exceeds a ~600 KB soft cap, flattening transparency first; images already compact are stored as-is, and if re-encoding doesn't shrink it the original is kept. Replaced the old "over 4 MB, store anyway?" confirm with a friendly post-add note. Verified in-browser: a 4.7 MB / 3000×2000 PNG → 1600px / ~73 KB JPEG, "Image optimized for local storage (4.7 MB → ~73 KB)" shown, 0 console errors.

items.title: Round out `.gitignore`
items.prompt: Add the remaining Cloudflare/Node dev-cruft patterns to `.gitignore`: `.mf/` (Miniflare), `*.local`, `npm-debug.log*`, `.pnpm-debug.log*`, `.cache/`. Don't remove existing entries.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** added `.mf/` (under Cloudflare), `npm-debug.log*` + `pnpm-debug.log*` (under logs), and a new "Local-only overrides & caches" section with `*.local` + `.cache/`. No existing entries removed.

items.title: Single-source nav, footer, app-shell, and design tokens via a tiny prebuild
items.prompt: Kill the structural duplication without adding a runtime/bundler. The nav and footer are copy-pasted across 6 HTML pages, the `:root` design tokens are declared in 3 files (`index.html`, `site.css`, `app/app.css`), and `app/staging.html` is a near-verbatim copy of `app/index.html`. Introduce a small Node prebuild script (run by hand and/or as the Pages build command) that injects shared partials (`partials/nav.html`, `partials/footer.html`, the shared app shell) into the HTML, and move the tokens into one `tokens.css` imported everywhere. Output must stay 100% static files served over http(s). Done when: nav/footer/tokens/app-shell each exist in exactly one source, the built pages are byte-equivalent in behavior, and the deploy is still static.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `tokens.css` is the lone source of design tokens — `site.css` + `app/app.css` `@import` it, the homepage links it (3 `:root` blocks → 1; verified `--accent`/bg resolve on info, home, and app pages). `partials/nav.html` + `partials/footer.html` are injected by `scripts/build-includes.mjs` (idempotent; `active=KEY` highlights the current link) into the four info pages via `<!-- include:nav/footer -->` markers. Footers were unified (each had drifted — self-link omission + a howto-only line, which was relocated into howto's body). Homepage + admin keep bespoke nav/footer by design (different links/CTA/scroll-spy), sharing only tokens. Verified in-browser: nav active states, unified footer, correct theme, 0 console errors; build re-run reports 0 changes. **Not done (deferred):** the app *shell* (`app/index.html`↔`staging.html` near-duplicate markup) is still hand-kept — fold into a shared shell partial in a follow-up (pairs naturally with A3).

items.title: Split `app/app.js` into concern-scoped script files
items.prompt: `app/app.js` is ~1,660 lines doing parsing, metrics, rendering, data-manager, filters, journal, and export. Split it into multiple plain `<script>` files loaded in order (e.g. `core.js` = state+metrics, `render.js` = dashboard, `datamanager.js`, `export.js`), no bundler. Preserve load order and globals. Done when: behavior is unchanged across main/demo/staging (verify in preview), each file has a clear single responsibility, and all JS passes `node --check`.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `app.js` → 7 ordered classic scripts: `core.js` (globals/helpers/metrics/format/cost/ refdata), `render.js` (cards/curve/calendar/advanced/break-even + scope/filter driver), `data.js` (import/demo/filters/journal/session/setup), `ui.js` (panels/staging flair/file-dl), `export.js` (report), `datamanager.js` (manage-data modal), `main.js` (event wiring + boot, loaded LAST). Cut at section boundaries in original order, so concatenation == original (no reordering). Verified: all 7 pass `node --check`; main app (initial state, 9 brokers), demo (loaded, curve), and staging (v0.13, 1539-row manage data, terminal, session pill) all boot with 0 console errors; cross-file fns resolve. Old app.js removed; 3 HTML pages load the 7 scripts in order. README updated. Note: staging code still lives in `ui.js` — fully isolating it is A3.

items.title: Move staging-only JS into its own file + decouple from shared code
items.prompt: Extract the staging-only logic (`initStaging`, activity terminal, session pill, workspace templates — ~120 lines, gated by `STAGING_PAGE`) into a `staging.js` that ONLY `staging.html` includes, so the main app can't be affected by staging experiments. Replace the `logAction()` calls sprinkled in shared code (`commitPending`, `dmExport`, etc.) with a lightweight event model: core emits events (`data:imported`, `note:saved`, `backup:created`, `trade:deleted`), staging.js subscribes. Main app and demo must not reference any staging symbol. Done when: removing `staging.js` leaves main+demo fully working, and the terminal still logs the same actions in staging.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** all staging flair (activity terminal, session pill, workspace templates, the staging resize redraw) moved from `ui.js` into a new `app/staging.js` that ONLY `staging.html` loads (inserted before `main.js`). `core.js` gained a tiny event bus (`emit`/`onEvent` over `EventTarget`); the `logAction()` calls in shared code (`data.js`, `datamanager.js`, `main.js`) were replaced with `emit('data:imported' | 'note:saved' | 'trade:deleted' | 'backup:created' | 'data:erased')`, and `boot()` now `emit('app:ready')` instead of calling `initStaging()`. `staging.js` subscribes and logs. `seedStagingIfEmpty` stays in `main.js` (boot-time data bootstrap; keeps the boot path robust). Verified in-browser: main app + demo load NO staging.js and define zero staging fns (boot fine, 0 console errors); staging's terminal logs session-ready + backup + delete via the bus. All 8 scripts pass `node --check`. Note: the staging↔index app-shell markup duplication (deferred from A1) is still open — now tracked as A5.

items.title: Keep the Store interface as the only persistence seam
items.prompt: Guardrail for future work: all reads/writes stay behind `Store` (`app/store.js`) so the planned cloud/ sync tier can drop in a `CloudStore` with the same async methods. Reject any change that calls `indexedDB` directly from app/render code.

items.title: Single-source the app shell (staging.html ↔ app/index.html ↔ demo.html)
items.prompt: Finish what A1 started: the three app pages share a near-identical shell (top bar, landing/setup, the dashboard panels, the data-manager modal) that's hand-kept in three files — `app/staging.html` is a near-verbatim copy of `app/index.html` and the biggest drift risk (A3 isolated the staging *JS*, not the markup). Extend the existing prebuild (`scripts/build-includes.mjs`) to inject a shared app-shell partial, with the per-page differences handled by markers/flags: `data-mode`, the version badge, the demo's trimmed top bar + disabled Manage-data, and the staging-only markup (session pill + popup, the filter Save/Saved controls, the activity-terminal panel). Output must stay 100% static. Done when: the shared top-bar / dashboard / modal markup lives in one source, the three app pages render identically to today (verify main/demo/staging in preview), and editing the shell updates all three.

items.title: Consolidate ad-hoc breakpoints into a named scale
items.prompt: There are ~15 distinct media breakpoints across `app/app.css`, `index.html`, and `site.css` (760/880/1180/620/1080/780/680/520/460/900/840/720/660/560… plus staging 1100/1480/1700). Define a small named scale (e.g. sm 560 / md 760 / lg 1080 / xl 1480) and migrate the queries to it, keeping the responsive-in-place approach (do NOT split mobile into separate files). Adjust only where a query doesn't already land near a scale value; don't regress current layouts. Done when: queries use the named scale, and main/demo/staging/marketing look the same or better at mobile/tablet/desktop/4K widths (spot-check in preview at a few widths).

items.title: Use wide-display real estate deliberately (4K)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** main app + demo `.wrap` now bumps to 1360px (≥1480) / 1520px (≥1920) — dashboard fills wide displays (verified composed at 1920px, side margins ~200px vs ~370px). **Deliberately did NOT widen the marketing homepage**: bumping its container pushed body paragraphs to ~1520px (~150 chars/line), hurting line-length. Possible follow-up: widen only the homepage's grid sections (features/pricing), leaving prose narrow. On very wide screens the marketing pages and main app cap narrow while staging goes edge-to-edge. Review the max-width caps and make the main app + key marketing sections use the extra width more intentionally on ≥1480px (without hurting readability/line length). Done when: at 4K the main app and homepage feel composed, not stranded in a narrow column.

items.title: (staging) Move the session indicator next to the wordmark
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** pill now sits after the "Blotterbook v0.13" version badge; wrapped pill + popup in a relatively-positioned `.sesswrap` so the legend popup stays anchored under the pill. Verified in-browser. In `app/staging.html` the session-status pill (`#sesspill`) sits at the far LEFT of the header, before the wordmark. Reposition it to the RIGHT of the "Blotterbook v0.13" text (after the `.ver` badge) so it reads "Blotterbook v0.13 · ●Online". Keep the click-to-explain popup working and the header left-aligned. Touch the `.brand` markup + `app.css`. Done when: the online/offline/degraded pill sits just right of the version badge, popup still opens, no mobile layout shift.

items.title: (staging) Move Reset filters next to Save filter
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** Reset filters now sits immediately after Save filter (dropped `fbtn`/margin-left:auto); the trade-count floats to the right of the bar via a new `.fpush` util. Verified adjacent + count right. In the staging filter bar (`app/staging.html` + `app.css`), the Reset filters button (`#f_reset`) is separated from Save filter (`#f_save`). Relocate Reset so it sits adjacent to Save filter in the filter module. Both must keep working. Done when: Save filter and Reset filters are next to each other in the filter bar; filtering + saving still work.

items.title: Orient readers of the multi-surface app scripts
items.prompt: (Updated post-A2: `app.js` was split into core/render/data/ui/export/datamanager/main.) Add a header block at the top of `app/core.js` documenting the `PAGE_MODE` matrix ('' main / 'demo' / 'staging'), what `STAGING_PAGE`/`DEMO_MODE` gate, which globals are staging-only, and the load order of the seven scripts. Add a one-line intent comment above the densest metrics-math one-liners (the Metrics section in `app/core.js`) and the equity-curve math in `app/render.js`. Comments only — no behavior change.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** added an orientation block at the top of `app/core.js` (7-script load order, `STAGING_PAGE`/ `DEMO_MODE` gating, shared-global-state map + "persist only via Store" note), and intent comments on the dense metrics math (realized max-drawdown walk, consecutive-streak loop, daily-PnL Sharpe) plus the equity-curve linear scales in `app/render.js`. Comments only — diff is 31 insertions/0 deletions; both files pass `node --check`.

items.title: Backlog module in the admin panel (migrate backlog.md → committed backlog.json)
items.prompt:
**Migrate the backlog's source of truth from `.claude/backlog.md` to a committed `data/backlog.json`.** This is NOT just adding a JSON alongside the markdown — `backlog.json` REPLACES `backlog.md` and becomes THE backlog we read and update from now on (the old gitignored `.md` is retired once migrated). Yes, it will be committed to the repo; that's intended and accepted, so the deployed admin page can read it.

`backlog.json` must contain ALL information currently in `backlog.md` — including the full per-item **prompt text** and the **"Shipped:" done-notes** — losing nothing in the migration. Suggested shape per item: `{id, title, category, effort, priority, status, completedDate, prompt, doneNote}`.

Then surface it in the admin panel (`admin.html`), behind the existing Cloudflare Access + admin-key protection: render the items grouped by category showing **category + effort + status only** — the `prompt`/`doneNote` text must NOT be shown in the admin UI element (it stays in the JSON data, just hidden from that view). Keep a persistent HISTORY of every completed item (don't drop them), and add a counts module: per category, completed vs remaining, plus an overall total. Done when: `data/backlog.json` is the single committed source (markdown retired) and carries every field incl. prompts; the admin page lists open + completed items by category with effort/status (no prompt text visible); completed history persists; and a per-category completed/remaining tally renders.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** migrated `.claude/backlog.md` → committed `data/backlog.json` (26 items, all fields incl. prompts + doneNotes) via a one-time parser, then retired the `.md` to a redirect stub. Added a read-only **Backlog** section to `admin.html` that fetches the JSON and renders per-category completed/remaining count cards + an overall tally + the grouped item list (id / title / effort / status badge). Verified in-browser: 8 categories, 26 rows, "15 done · 10 remaining · 1 guardrail · 60% complete", and that the prompt/doneNote text is NOT present in the rendered admin DOM. 0 console errors.

items.title: (staging) Workspace dropdown reverts to default; remove the Default button
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** placeholder renamed "— Workspace —" → "— Default —"; selecting it now clears the saved order/collapsed state and resets to `DEFAULT_DASH_ORDER`. Removed the standalone Default button. Verified: scrambled layout reverts on selecting "— Default —". Covers two related staging requests. (1) Rename the workspace-template `<select>` placeholder from "— Workspace —" to "— Default —", and make SELECTING it revert the layout to default (today the placeholder is a no-op; the separate Default button does the revert). (2) Remove the now-redundant `#ws_default` "Default" button. Touch `app/staging.html` + the staging UI handlers (`ui.js` `initStaging` / the `ws_*` wiring). Done when: choosing "— Default —" resets module order + collapsed state to default; the standalone Default button is gone; Save layout and loading saved templates still work.

items.title: (staging) Export-report popup: preview + multi-format download + email
items.prompt: Replace staging's "Export report" (which auto-opens a new tab) with a modal popup (reuse the app's modal pattern): a scrollable PREVIEW of the report plus Download / Email a copy / Close buttons. Beside Download, a format `<select>` — "Choose format" (default selected), PDF, PNG, JPEG, MD — with Download DISABLED until a real format is chosen. PDF = the existing print-to-PDF path; PNG/JPEG = rasterize the report node to canvas; MD = generate a Markdown summary. "Email a copy" keeps the current mailto behavior (prefilled draft). Build it staging-only (gated by `STAGING_PAGE`) so the main app + demo Export are untouched until promoted. Done when: Export report opens the popup with a live preview; Download is greyed until a format is picked, then exports the chosen format; Email opens a prefilled mailto; Close dismisses; main app + demo Export unchanged.

items.title: (staging) Edit/Delete buttons overlap the PnL column in the trades table
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** root cause was `display:flex` on a `<td>` (dropped it out of the table column model and overlapped PnL). Reverted to a normal table-cell; space the buttons with margin. Verified: action cell starts exactly at the PnL cell's right edge, no overlap. (Fix is in shared `app.css`, applies app-wide.) In the Manage-data trades table, a per-trade row's Edit/Delete action buttons visually overlay the PnL number (the actions render on top of the PnL column). Fix the table/cell layout in `app.css` (and the row markup in `app/datamanager.js` if needed) so the actions occupy their own column with no overlap. Check at staging fullscreen widths where it's most visible. Done when: PnL and the Edit/Delete actions are cleanly separated across widths; Edit/Delete still work.

items.title: (staging) Activity Terminal should fill its module (no negative space)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `.panel.term .pbody` is now a growing flex column and the terminal flexes to fill it (160px min on narrow). Verified at 1800px (3-col): terminal grows to 664px to fill its 777px panel. The Activity Terminal console (`#termwin`) has a fixed height, leaving empty space below it inside the panel on the wide/fullscreen staging grid. Make the terminal window fill its module body (flex / height:100%) so there's no gap, keeping the scroll + 200-line cap. `app.css` (`.panel.term` / `.termwin`). Done when: in fullscreen staging the terminal fills its panel with no negative space; scroll + autoscroll still work.

items.title: Second full repo audit
items.prompt: Read-only audit (NO code edits) of the current repo: architecture, code quality, duplicated code, and security posture — accounting for everything shipped since the first audit (symbol sanitization, `_headers` CSP, `tokens.css` + nav/footer partials, the `app.js` → 7-script split, screenshot downscaling). Give concrete, prioritized findings, and append any new actions to this backlog in the same format. Done when: a written, prioritized audit is delivered and new follow-ups are added to the backlog.

items.title: Roadmap rework: customer-facing, big-picture
items.prompt: Rework `roadmap.html` for a CUSTOMER audience evaluating the product — not us tracking tasks. Today it mirrors this backlog (granular/internal). Reframe into big-picture, benefit-oriented themes: shipped capabilities, what's in progress, and what's planned — feature-level, no infra/engineering minutiae. Keep the shared nav/footer/tokens (it uses the partials + `tokens.css`). The engineering backlog stays separate (this file / the F1 admin module). Done when: roadmap.html reads as a customer-facing shipped / in-progress / planned feature story, distinct from the engineering backlog.

items.title: (staging) Design review: per-day vs per-trade notes & data model
items.prompt: Design review (mostly thinking + a written recommendation; minimal code) of how per-DAY and per-TRADE data fit together. Today the calendar "day notes" is a bare comment box, while Manage data has richer per-trade notes + screenshots — inconsistent and not streamlined. Also the Manage-data "Day notes" list shows just "note" for every entry (uninformative — should preview the text/date). Propose a cleaner unified model for per-day vs per-trade notes/attachments and how each surfaces in the UI; capture the recommendation and add concrete follow-up backlog items. Done when: a written recommendation exists for streamlining per-day vs per-trade notes (incl. fixing the "note"-only day-notes list), with next-step items added to the backlog.

items.title: Review "Definitions & Caveats" for accuracy
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the only stale item was "Trade = one row … exactly as TradingView exported it" — rewrote it to "Trade = one closed position" covering auto-detection, one-row-per-close vs FIFO fills-pairing (+ hold time), and the beta caveat. Applied to all 3 app pages (identical panel). Rest verified accurate. Re-read the dashboard's "Definitions & Caveats" panel against the app's CURRENT behavior and correct anything stale — metric definitions, scope behavior, cost/tax model wording, and any staging-only features. Accuracy pass only (content). Done when: every definition/caveat matches current functionality; corrections applied.

items.title: Rename `app/index.html` → `app/app.html`
items.prompt: Rename `app/index.html` to `app/app.html`. IMPORTANT URL impact: the app is currently served at `/app/` (directory index = index.html); after the rename `/app/` has no index. Either update every internal Launch link (`/app/` → `/app/app.html`) across the marketing pages + admin + README, OR add a Cloudflare Pages redirect/rewrite so `/app/` still resolves to `app.html` — pick one and apply it consistently. Relative `app.css`/script paths are unaffected; `demo.html` + `staging.html` are already named, so only the main entry changes. Done when: the app loads at its new path, every Launch link works (no dead `/app/` route), and the README reflects the new filename.

howToDrive: One item per prompt; one surface per prompt where possible.
Lead with the *goal*, not the mechanism; let me pick the workable path.
If you batch, name the 1–3 must-haves in case I run low on budget.
Keep using the change-plan + memory pattern for any multi-step item.
